display.purpose: Schibsted Grotesk 800. Product name, page titles, boldest headings. Tight leading, never above ~6rem.

body.purpose: Hanken Grotesk 400-500. All reading copy, 65-75ch, Ebony or Graphite only.

label.purpose: Hanken Grotesk 650, 11px, 0.12em uppercase. Metadata keys and control-group labels, one per cluster.

mono.purpose: JetBrains Mono 500 with tabular figures. Every time, tempo, bar:beat, count, and timecode.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Default elevation for raised cards and panels (.surface). Warm-tinted, low contrast.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Hover / active / popovers. Earned by interaction, never default.

shadows.name: inset-hairline
shadows.purpose: Flat nested surfaces (.surface-flat); edge without elevation.

motion.name: pulse
motion.purpose: In-progress status (Processing/Importing) opacity pulse on the StatusDot.

components.name: Primary Pill
components.description: The default action button: Ebony fill, Spruce text, optional inset icon dot. Lifts on hover.

components.name: Accent Pill
components.description: The single primary action per surface. Rosin fill, near-white text. Use sparingly (The Rosin Rule).

components.name: Ghost Pill
components.description: Secondary action: transparent with a hairline inset border, Ebony text. Gains a Maple background on hover.
components.css: .ds-pill-ghost{display:inline-flex;height:44px;align-items:center;justify-content:center;border:0;border-radius:999px;background:transparent;color:var(--ink,#2a2926);box-shadow:inset 0 0 0 1.5px rgba(42,41,38,.16);padding:0 20px;font:600 14px/1 'Hanken Grotesk',sans-serif;cursor:pointer;transition:background .2s ease,box-shadow .2s ease}.ds-pill-ghost:hover{background:var(--card,#fbf9f5);box-shadow:inset 0 0 0 1.5px rgba(42,41,38,.16),0 1px 2px rgba(40,38,33,.05),0 8px 24px rgba(40,38,33,.06)}.ds-pill-ghost:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent,#c2773a);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Status (label + dot)
components.description: The signature status readout: a colored dot ALWAYS paired with its text label. In-progress states pulse. Color is never shown alone.

components.name: Live Chip
components.description: Verdigris-tinted chip marking a ready/live state. Pairs with a text label.

components.name: Segmented Nav
components.description: The primary nav control: an Ebony track holding pill segments; the active one is a Spruce pill.

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: A raised Maple panel on the Spruce field, resting elevation, 14px radius. No border; the shadow carries the edge.
components.html:
Song structure
Intro · Verse · Chorus · Bridge · Outro

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Pill-shaped Maple field with a hairline inset and a leading icon. Rosin focus ring; Ash placeholder.
components.html: <label class="ds-input-wrap"><svg class="ds-input-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="8"></circle><path d="m21 21-4.3-4.3"></path></svg><input class="ds-input" placeholder="Search songs" /></label>

components.name: Cover Art
components.description: Signature procedural cover: deterministic concentric rings over a dark gradient, hue driven by a hash of the song id. Stable, distinct, on-brand placeholder.
components.css: .ds-cover{position:relative;display:block;width:64px;height:64px;flex-shrink:0;overflow:hidden;border-radius:12px;background:radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, oklch(0.74 0.11 var(--cover-hue,55)) 0 7%, transparent 7.5%),repeating-radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, transparent 0 18%, oklch(0.75 0.06 var(--cover-hue,55) / 0.18) 18.5% 19.5%, transparent 20% 25%),linear-gradient(145deg, oklch(0.3 0.04 var(--cover-hue,55)), oklch(0.18 0.02 var(--cover-hue,55)))}

narrative.overview: WereCode looks and feels like a stringed-instrument maker's bench: a warm, pale work surface with exact tools laid out, lit by a low lamp, where the craft is taken seriously and the noise is kept out. The page is planed Spruce, faintly grained with a 4px dotted texture so it reads as a real surface rather than a flat screen. Type is set in confident grotesk; numbers are cut in monospace with tabular figures so a ticking transport never jitters. The single warm accent is Rosin amber, used the way a luthier reaches for one varnish: sparingly, for the thing that matters right now. A second signal, Verdigris, the blue-green patina of aged brass tuning pegs, means live / ready / playing. The tension the system lives on is precision against warmth: the mono transport and hairline-exact panels earn a musician's trust, while the paper grain, soft warm shadows, and rounded hand-sized pill controls lower the intimidation of pulling a song apart.
keyCharacteristics: Light, warm Spruce field with a faint dotted-paper grain; never dark mode, never stark white.
One rare warm accent (Rosin), one live signal (Verdigris), everything else neutral wood tones.
Monospace, tabular figures for every time / tempo / count.
Tactile, rounded controls that lift softly on touch; never flat, never industrial.
Status is always color plus a text label, never color alone.

rules.name: The Rosin Rule
rules.body: Rosin is varnish, not paint. It appears on the single most important action on a surface, on focus, and on in-progress state, and nowhere else. If two things on screen are Rosin, one of them is wrong. Large Rosin fills are forbidden.

rules.name: The Two-Signal Rule
rules.body: Rosin means 'your move / in progress.' Verdigris means 'live / ready / playing.' They are never swapped and never used decoratively.

rules.name: The Tabular Transport Rule
rules.body: Every digit that can change while playing, the playhead time, tempo, bar:beat, counts, is JetBrains Mono with tabular figures. The readout must not reflow as it ticks.

rules.name: The Quiet-Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: The uppercase label is a metadata key and a control-group label, not a decorative kicker stacked above every section. One per cluster.

rules.name: The Lift-on-Touch Rule
rules.body: A surface sits at Resting elevation and only climbs to Lifted as a response to interaction. Elevation is feedback, not decoration. Nested surfaces use the inset hairline and cast no shadow.

rules.name: The Labeled-Status Rule
rules.body: Status is never color alone; a StatusDot always carries its text label, and motion (pulse) reinforces in-progress.

dos: Do keep the page on the warm Spruce field with its faint dotted grain. Light mode is the doctrine.
Do spend Rosin like varnish: one primary action, focus, and in-progress only.
Do keep the Two-Signal contract: Rosin = your move / in progress, Verdigris = live / ready / playing.
Do set every time, tempo, and count in JetBrains Mono with tabular figures.
Do pair every status color with a text label and use pulse for in-progress.
Do keep body text at Ebony or Graphite; treat Ash as placeholder-only.
Do let surfaces rest flat-ish and lift softly on touch; warm-tinted shadows only.
donts: Don't build a cluttered legacy DAW: no wall-of-knobs density, no gray engineer-only panels.
Don't make it a toy / gamified app: no badges, confetti, cartoon mascots, or streak theater.
Don't drift to generic AI SaaS: no purple gradients, no glass cards, no hero-metric template, no 'supercharge / seamless' copy.
Don't go sterile corporate: no cold navy-and-gray data grids.
Don't rely on color alone to carry meaning; always add label, shape, or position.
Don't use gradient text, colored side-stripe borders, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't put proportional numerals in the transport, or let a ticking readout reflow.
Don't flip to dark mode or a stark #fff background; the warmth is the brand.
Don't nest a shadowed card inside another shadowed card; use the inset-hairline flat surface.
